description: v1.1.0 perf baseline (P3-T11). Captured against the stress-test-5k fixture (5000 ConfigMaps + 1000 Deployments + 1000 owner-ref ReplicaSets + 200 Services) across Tier 1 (in-memory) and Tier 2 (PostgreSQL + Apache AGE), on Docker Desktop Kubernetes under WSL2. Tier 2 numbers go through kubectl port-forward + WSL2 networking (~10ms/round-trip vs native Linux); Tier 1 is benched against a local memory-backed process. v1.1 reworked the informer edge-extraction path to issue namespace-scoped store queries instead of snapshotting the whole graph on every event — see CHANGELOG and the perf(extractor) commit.

spec_targets._comment: Targets carried forward unchanged from perf-baseline-v1.0.json — they are workload-size based (5K+ resources), not release based. cluster_view + namespace_view scale O(R) on the namespace resource count and the JSON marshal of the response dominates wall time; blast-radius stays at 500ms because the recursive-CTE traversal is bounded by the affected subgraph, not the total graph. Re-tightening the targets is P3-T15's call, not P3-T11's.
